sections.paragraph:
σιδηροῦς, ᾶ, οῦν (s. prec. entry; Hom.+ in the form σιδήρεος, whereas the Attic Gks. have the contracted form. The word is also found in ins, pap, LXX; TestJud 9:40; JosAs 10:5; GrBar 3:7; ApcEsdr 4:25 p. 28, 30 Tdf.; Philo, Op. M. 141; Joseph. [in both forms; cp. Schmidt, Joseph. 492]; SibOr 3, 540) (made of) iron of a bar B 11:4 (Is 45:2; JosAs 10:5; ApcEsdr 4:25). Of a prison door (s. πύλη a) Ac 12:10. Of breastplates Rv 9:9. In imagery = merciless (Hom.+; cp. Περὶ ὕψους 13, 1 after Pla., Rep. 586a σιδηροῖς κέρασι) ῥάβδος σιδηρᾶ (after Ps 2:9) Rv 2:27; 12:5; 19:15 (ποιμαίνω 2aγ).—DELG s.v. σίδηρος. M-M s.v. σιδήρεος.

prec. prec. = preceding

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

TestJud TestJud = Testament of Judah, s. Test12Patr—List 2

JosAs JosAs = Joseph and Aseneth—Lists 2, 5

GrBar GrBar = Greek Baruch (Apocalypsis Baruchi Graece)—List 2

ApcEsdr ApcEsdr = Apocalypse of Esdras—List 2

Tdf. Tdf. = CvTischendorf—List 1, beg.

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Joseph Joseph This abbr. used when no specific texts are cited (s. Jos.); I a.d.—List 5

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Schmidt, Joseph. Schmidt, Joseph. = WS., De Flavii Iosephi elocutione—List 6

SibOr SibOr = Oracula Sibyllina, II–III a.d.—List 5

B B = Barnabas (the Letter of), II a.d., except in series of uncial witnesses, in which case B refers to Codex Vaticanus (s. also Vat.). When the abbrv. B would ambiguous, Vat. is used for the codex.—List 1

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 923
